dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 16 responding for a cardiac arrest.
Hi, I'm Mark, an EMT with the rescue squad. What's the situation with the patient?
He was unconscious and not breathing when I arrived here in the taxi.
Ma'am, can you open your eyes for me and squeeze my hand?
No response. Pupils appear fixed and dilated.
Ma'am, can you hear me? I'm going to gently check you for response.
No movement. Slumped over in the back seat. Appears pulseless and apneic.
Partner, prepare BVM. I'm checking the airway for patency.
Respirations: No spontaneous breathing observed. Unresponsive to stimulation.
SpO2 is reading 0%. No oxygen saturation detected.
Pulse is absent. Blood pressure appears zero. No peripheral pulses detected.
Radial and carotid pulses absent. Patient is pulseless.
Blood pressure: No signs of a pulse or waveform.
Ma'am, can you tell us anything about your condition or what led to this?
He has a history of hypertension, diabetes, CHF, and sleep apnea.
I picked him up and he seemed okay at first. The ride was only 10 minutes.
Blood glucose is 283 mg/dL, well above the normal range.
EKG shows asystole. Confirmed, no cardiac activity.
Capnography reading 60-65 during ongoing ventilations.
Bilateral pupils are fixed at about 8mm. GCS of 3.
Abdomen slightly distended. No signs of spontaneous movement.
His medications include Humulin N, metformin, Clonidine, Cozaar, aspirin, Vistaril, Zofran, Advair, and Ezetimibe. Shrimp allergy only.
Initiating CPR. AutoPulse is ready, starting now.
Rhythm is still asystole after setting up 4-lead monitoring.
Intraosseous access successfully established at right humeral head using aseptic technique.
EKG waveform remains flat, asystole unchanged.
Epinephrine 1:10,000, 1.0mg administered via IO. No response noted.
Manual compressions and BVM with 15LPM oxygen started, capnography ongoing.
Respiratory effort unchanged. Still at 0 respirations per minute.
King LT/I-gel airway placed, secured with tube holder and capnography.
Rhythm remains asystole, no change during airway management.
Narcan 2.0mg administered intraosseously. Patient status remains unchanged.
Sodium Bicarbonate 50mEq IO done per protocol. Patient response: no change.
Maintaining airway with NPA. Continue ventilations and monitoring.
Rechecked vitals. Pulse is still absent. All readings confirm no response.
Second dose of 1.0mg Epinephrine given with no clinical improvement.
Asystole remains. Capnography stable at ~65. Ventilations continue.
No pain observed at baseline. No pain reported as a symptom.
Patient transferred from the car to the stretcher in a supine position.
Fully secured on the stretcher. IV maintained with saline lock.
Transport hot to [COMPANY_NAME]. Active resuscitation ongoing.
All resuscitation equipment is secured, ready for transport.
All systems verified and ready. Transport is set to begin smoothly.
Transferring a 41-year-old female found non-responsive and apneic in the back of the taxi.
PMH: HTN, Diabetes (Insulin-dependent), CHF, Sleep Apnea. Shrimp allergy.
Full cardiac arrest protocol (2-7) applied. No response to all actions performed.
Treatments include four Epinephrine doses, 2mg Narcan, 50mEq Sodium Bicarbonate, 500cc NS via bag.
Resuscitation ongoing during transport with no improvement in vitals or status observed.
Patient status unchanged. Slight abdominal distention noted.
Rhythm remains asystole. No response to all interventions. Pupils fixed and dilated.
Handoff to ED complete. Equipment, documentation, and patient are accounted for.
